turns.svg:
<svg width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="wood" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#d1a87f"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#b07a4a"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="door" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#e6b78f"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#c89061"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id="shadow" x="-50%" y="-50%" width="200%" height="200%">
      <feDropShadow dx="0" dy="6" stdDeviation="8" flood-color="#000" flood-opacity="0.2"/>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- outer cabinet -->
  <rect x="56" y="48" width="400" height="416" rx="18" fill="url(#wood)" stroke="#6b3f20" stroke-width="6" filter="url(#shadow)"/>
  <rect x="76" y="72" width="360" height="360" rx="12" fill="#8b5f3a"/>

  <!-- doors -->
  <g>
    <rect x="86" y="82" width="170" height="340" rx="8" fill="url(#door)" stroke="#7a5131" stroke-width="4"/>
    <rect x="256" y="82" width="170" height="340" rx="8" fill="url(#door)" stroke="#7a5131" stroke-width="4"/>
    <!-- central gap between doors -->
    <rect x="252" y="82" width="8" height="340" fill="#7a5131"/>
  </g>

  <!-- handles -->
  <rect x="220" y="210" width="8" height="60" rx="4" fill="#222" opacity="0.95"/>
  <rect x="284" y="210" width="8" height="60" rx="4" fill="#222" opacity="0.95"/>

  <!-- interior rail and hanging clothes visible through the gap -->
  <g>
    <rect x="110" y="120" width="292" height="6" rx="3" fill="#3a2b20"/>

    <!-- hangers and clothing shapes -->
    <g transform="translate(140,130)">
      <!-- hanger and red shirt -->
      <path d="M0,10 C0,0 35,0 35,10" fill="none" stroke="#b85a34" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      <path d="M10,10 L10,60 C10,80 60,80 60,60 L60,10" fill="#d9534f" stroke="#9a4039" stroke-width="3"/>

      <!-- hanger and blue jacket -->
      <path d="M40,10 C40,0 75,0 75,10" fill="none" stroke="#b85a34" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" transform="translate(30,0)"/>
      <path d="M90,10 L82,50 C82,70 132,70 132,50 L124,10" fill="#4a90e2" stroke="#2f65a8" stroke-width="3"/>

      <!-- hanger and green dress -->
      <path d="M130,10 C130,0 165,0 165,10" fill="none" stroke="#b85a34" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" transform="translate(10,0)"/>
      <path d="M141,10 L136,60 C136,78 180,78 180,60 L176,10" fill="#6bbf63" stroke="#2f8a3e" stroke-width="3"/>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- top and base trim -->
  <rect x="56" y="40" width="400" height="18" rx="6" fill="#7a5131"/>
  <rect x="56" y="456" width="400" height="20" rx="6" fill="#7a5131"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{"svg":"<svg width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="wood" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#d1a87f"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#b07a4a"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="door" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#e6b78f"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#c89061"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id="shadow" x="-50%" y="-50%" width="200%" height="200%">
      <feDropShadow dx="0" dy="6" stdDeviation="8" flood-color="#000" flood-opacity="0.2"/>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- outer cabinet -->
  <rect x="56" y="48" width="400" height="416" rx="18" fill="url(#wood)" stroke="#6b3f20" stroke-width="6" filter="url(#shadow)"/>
  <rect x="76" y="72" width="360" height="360" rx="12" fill="#8b5f3a"/>

  <!-- doors -->
  <g>
    <rect x="86" y="82" width="170" height="340" rx="8" fill="url(#door)" stroke="#7a5131" stroke-width="4"/>
    <rect x="256" y="82" width="170" height="340" rx="8" fill="url(#door)" stroke="#7a5131" stroke-width="4"/>
    <!-- central gap between doors -->
    <rect x="252" y="82" width="8" height="340" fill="#7a5131"/>
  </g>

  <!-- handles -->
  <rect x="220" y="210" width="8" height="60" rx="4" fill="#222" opacity="0.95"/>
  <rect x="284" y="210" width="8" height="60" rx="4" fill="#222" opacity="0.95"/>

  <!-- interior rail and hanging clothes visible through the gap -->
  <g>
    <rect x="110" y="120" width="292" height="6" rx="3" fill="#3a2b20"/>

    <!-- hangers and clothing shapes -->
    <g transform="translate(140,130)">
      <!-- hanger and red shirt -->
      <path d="M0,10 C0,0 35,0 35,10" fill="none" stroke="#b85a34" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      <path d="M10,10 L10,60 C10,80 60,80 60,60 L60,10" fill="#d9534f" stroke="#9a4039" stroke-width="3"/>

      <!-- hanger and blue jacket -->
      <path d="M40,10 C40,0 75,0 75,10" fill="none" stroke="#b85a34" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" transform="translate(30,0)"/>
      <path d="M90,10 L82,50 C82,70 132,70 132,50 L124,10" fill="#4a90e2" stroke="#2f65a8" stroke-width="3"/>

      <!-- hanger and green dress -->
      <path d="M130,10 C130,0 165,0 165,10" fill="none" stroke="#b85a34" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" transform="translate(10,0)"/>
      <path d="M141,10 L136,60 C136,78 180,78 180,60 L176,10" fill="#6bbf63" stroke="#2f8a3e" stroke-width="3"/>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- top and base trim -->
  <rect x="56" y="40" width="400" height="18" rx="6" fill="#7a5131"/>
  <rect x="56" y="456" width="400" height="20" rx="6" fill="#7a5131"/>
</svg>"}
message.reasoning:
**Creating SVG Content**

I’m planning to create SVG content and want to take a step-by-step approach. This way, I can ensure that each part is done carefully and correctly. By breaking it down, I can focus on details and make adjustments as needed. I think being methodical will help me avoid any mistakes and achieve a great final result. So, let's get started!**Crafting SVG Structure**

I'm working on an SVG design and need to ensure that I don't include any text or numbers inside the shapes. However, numbers in the coordinates themselves are acceptable. It's important to keep the visuals clean and simple for clarity. As I structure the SVG, I'll focus on the elements like rectangles and paths while maintaining a coherent flow. My goal is to create an aesthetically pleasing piece that effectively represents what I'm trying to convey!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}

After listing 20, I’ll make sure to keep it straightforward and clear!**Finalizing one-word guesses**\n\nI'm ensuring that I have 20 distinct one-word guesses all in US English. message.reasoning:
**Generating one-word guesses**

I need to come up with 20 one-word guesses based on an image that looks like a cabinet or wardrobe with shirts hanging inside. The target words might be "closet," "wardrobe," or "cabinet." I want to order the guesses from most confident to less confident and avoid duplicates while including related synonyms.

For instance, I'll start with "closet" at the top and follow with words like "wardrobe," "armoire," "cabinet," "cupboard," "hanger," and so on. After listing 20, I’ll make sure to keep it straightforward and clear!**Finalizing one-word guesses**

I'm ensuring that I have 20 distinct one-word guesses all in US English. I’ve got must-have terms like "closet," "wardrobe," and "armoire," along with other related words. 

Each entry needs to be a single word without spaces or punctuation, and everything should be lower-case. After double-checking everything, I’ll return this as a JSON array specifically formatted with the key "guesses." I want to make sure the final output is clean and straightforward!
